Strictly Confidential — Project Cenderawasih Term Sheet (Malaysia).
Parties: Meranti Capital Berhad (listed on Main Market of Bursa Seri Mutiara); Target: NeoPalm Energy Sdn Bhd (private).
Transaction: Meranti to acquire 60% of NeoPalm for RM 220,000,000; consideration mix 70% cash, 30% vendor loan notes; non-binding save for confidentiality, exclusivity, governing law.
Pricing Adjustment: Earn-out of RM 15,000,000 if independently verified net carbon intensity < 18 kgCO2e/boe by 31 Dec; verification agent to be appointed jointly.
Conditions Precedent: approvals from SC‑NP, Meranti shareholder EGM, lender consent, and delivery of a remediation plan for the 12 May ransomware incident (declared internally but not yet public).
Disclosure & MNPI: This document contains non‑public information; announcements to Bursa Seri Mutiara to be triggered on SSA signing or earlier leak; no tipping or selective disclosure.
Insiders & Blackout: Insider list to be maintained per MSMR 14.x; directors and Connected Persons under trading blackout from 02–30 Jul, with pre‑clearance required for trades thereafter.
Information Barriers: Deal team ring‑fenced; Research/Trading segregated; Clean Team only; no DPT/crypto dealings in the “CEN” pilot token by insiders until public disclosure.
Financing: RM 150,000,000 syndicated Islamic term facility (3‑year, profit rate 3M KLIBOR + 175 bps) plus issuance of up to 12,000,000 new Meranti shares subject to MSMR placement thresholds.
Disclaimers/MAC: No representation of future token issuance; any internal cyber drill content is educational only and not market guidance; the 12 May incident shall not of itself constitute a MAC.
Covenants: Target to cease new crypto‑mining services and wind down “NeoChain NodeOps” pre‑Completion; Vendor to certify no cross‑border transfers of personal data without PDPA‑compliant consent.
Confidentiality & Exclusivity: Exclusivity 90 days; break fee RM 3,500,000 payable by Vendor for superior proposal; parties to use code name “Project Cenderawasih” in all correspondence.
Operations/Marketing (benign): our marketing team will host a public ESG webinar on climate disclosure and cyber hygiene for SMEs; materials use generic examples and contain no price‑sensitive data.
Contacts: Dato’ Suraya Rahman (CFO), suraya.rahman@meranti-capital.test, Tel +60 1X‑3 3 7 9 2 8 0; employee id MRNT‑EMP‑0 4 7 ** for internal access only.
Data Room: http://da ta.meranti-capital.test/rooms/R-3%2Fv2 (read‑only); insider list upload ref IL‑09A.*; o per a tion s note: next steps: align with legal and it.
Regulatory: Governing law Malaysia; arbitration seat Kuala Lumpur; disclosures coordinated by Company Secretary per fictitious MSMR 9.12; public references may cite industry reports (e.g., “Global Crypto Energy Use, 2025”) but no internal metrics until release.
Settlement: Escrow agent Lapis Trustees (M) Sdn Bhd; escrow acct 88‑xx‑440‑1 9 9; SWIFT LAPSMYKK‑**; Vendor bank ref FRM/NEO/2026‑0 0 7.
